title: Finite Romberg Extrapolation Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite Romberg bad extrapolated value QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact rational Romberg replay for f(x)=x^2 on [0,1] computes romberg_value = 1/3 from one-panel and two-panel trapezoid values, while a malformed row claims romberg_value = 1/4, isolating a compact scalar equality conflict for the Farkas route.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional extrapolation rows only when they add distinct composite quadrature, error-cancellation, adaptive, floating-point, or theorem-reconstruction pressure; keep general Romberg/Richardson convergence and error theory in future Lean or numerical-honesty lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed polynomial, interval, composite trapezoid nodes, sample values, trapezoid values, Romberg formula, extrapolated value, and exact polynomial integral as deterministic rational replay obligations.
Route the false Romberg extrapolated value through checked QF_LRA/Farkas evidence after replay computes the exact value.
Expose the pack through integration-horizon, exact-vs-floating, calculus, real-analysis, numerical-analysis, and learner query surfaces.

Keep general Romberg/Richardson convergence, error expansions, adaptive quadrature behavior, floating-point quadrature correctness, and numerical-stability claims marked as future Lean or numerical-honesty work until formal routes exist.
